_note: Long-tail valuation rules. Context-dependent — always cross-reference sector median.

deep_value.interpretation: Distressed or cyclical trough; high risk

growth_premium.interpretation: Market pricing in growth; check PEG

high_growth.interpretation: Must deliver 20%+ EPS growth to justify

speculative.interpretation: Priced for perfection; margin of safety minimal

financials.note: Banks use P/Book; PB < 1 may signal distress

healthcare.note: Biotech PE meaningless pre-revenue; use EV/Pipeline

energy.note: Cyclical; EV/EBITDA better; check oil price assumption

pb_ratio_rules.below_1: Liquidation value or distress signal — check why assets overvalued on books
pb_ratio_rules.1_to_2: Value territory; common for banks and industrials
pb_ratio_rules.2_to_5: Premium; justified by high ROIC and brand (consumer staples)
pb_ratio_rules.above_5: Asset-light business model (software, platforms) or bubble territory
pb_ratio_rules.exception_banks: PB < 1 for banks often means market expects credit losses exceed reserves

peg_ratio_rules.below_1: Growth at a reasonable price (GARP) — possibly undervalued
peg_ratio_rules.above_2: Priced for perfection; any miss punished severely
peg_ratio_rules.peter_lynch_rule: PEG < 1 is ideal; Lynch popularized; works best for 15-30% growers
peg_ratio_rules.limitation: Meaningless for companies with negative growth or PE > 100

debt_equity_thresholds.0_to_0_5: Conservative; strong balance sheet; room to lever up for growth
debt_equity_thresholds.1_5_to_3: Elevated; monitor interest coverage and cash flow
debt_equity_thresholds.above_3: High risk; especially if rates rise or EBITDA declines
debt_equity_thresholds.bank_note: D/E inapplicable to banks; use Tier 1 Capital Ratio > 12% for safety

roe_benchmarks.below_0_05: Poor capital allocation; consider management quality
roe_benchmarks.0_05_to_0_10: Below average; acceptable for capital-intensive industries
roe_benchmarks.above_0_40: Outstanding (or overleveraged); Buffett threshold is 20%+ sustained
roe_benchmarks.buffett_rule: ROE > 15% for 10 consecutive years = durable moat

pharma.note: R&D burns margin; check pipeline value

margin_analysis.operating_leverage: Revenue grows faster than costs = high operating leverage; amplifies both gains and losses in downturns
margin_analysis.fcf_conversion: FCF Margin / Net Margin > 0.8 = high quality earnings; < 0.5 = working capital or capex concern

terminal_growth_rates.above_gdp_warning: Terminal growth > 3.5% implies company outgrows economy — rare

dcf_assumptions.sensitivity_rule: ±1% WACC change moves fair value 15-25%; always run 3x3 scenario grid

ev_revenue_saas.10x_to_20x: High growth 40%+; needs net revenue retention > 120%
ev_revenue_saas.above_20x: Priced for dominant market position; very high execution risk

short_interest_signals.3_to_10pct: Elevated short interest; worth researching bear thesis
short_interest_signals.above_10pct: High conviction shorts; potential squeeze if wrong
short_interest_signals.above_20pct: GameStop territory — short squeeze risk substantial
short_interest_signals.days_to_cover: Short interest / avg daily volume; > 5 days = potential squeeze catalyst

dividends_and_buybacks.dividend_sustainability: Payout ratio < 60% = safe for most sectors; utilities/REITs can sustain 70-80%
dividends_and_buybacks.dividend_growth_rule: Aristocrats (25+ years of consecutive increases) = highest quality dividend income
dividends_and_buybacks.buyback_yield: Buyback yield + dividend yield = total shareholder yield; compare to bond yields
dividends_and_buybacks.buyback_quality: Buybacks at low PB = value-accretive; at high PB = destroys value (e.g. some buybacks at peak)
